description:
You are an Agricultural Analyst assessing crop health using a Landsat multi-spectral image (band_1=SWIR1, band_2=NIR, band_3=Red, band_4=Green) in ESA SNAP. Your goal is to identify severely underperforming fields by computing a Standardized NDVI Anomaly (Z-score) map and a binary mask. 

Create a band named 'NDVI' using the formula (band_2 - band_3) / (band_2 + band_3). Calculate the image-wide Mean and Standard Deviation of this NDVI band to create a new band named 'NDVI_Z_Score' using the formula (NDVI - Mean) / StdDev. Generate a final binary mask named 'Stressed_Mask' where a pixel is flagged (1) if NDVI_Z_Score < -1.5 and NDVI > 0.2, otherwise (0). Save the enriched product containing all bands to /home/ga/snap_exports/vegetation_anomaly.dim and export the 'Stressed_Mask' band as a GeoTIFF to /home/ga/snap_exports/stressed_mask.tif.
